title: IDF Airstrikes Kill 6 Including Child at Police Checkpoints in Southern Khan Younis
detail: Israeli forces struck two police checkpoints in southern Khan Younis on March 29, killing at least six Palestinians including a child and wounding several others. A separate IDF strike killed two Palestinian brothers near the Showa roundabout in Gaza City's Shejaiya neighborhood. At least 691 Palestinians have been killed since the October 2025 ceasefire took effect. The IDF described the strikes as targeting Hamas-affiliated armed personnel; Palestinian officials condemned the strikes as attacks on civilian infrastructure. The Gaza Health Ministry confirmed the casualties and transferred victims to Nasser Hospital. Al Jazeera reported the strikes as part of a pattern of continued Israeli military operations in Gaza during the Iran war period, with international attention focused elsewhere.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — US-Israel war on Iran: Day 30, March 29, 2026

title: Iranian Ballistic Missile Strikes Ne'ot Hovav Chemical Industrial Zone in the Negev
detail: An Iranian ballistic missile evaded Israeli air defense systems and struck the Ne'ot Hovav (Ramat Hovav) chemical industrial zone located approximately 12 kilometers south of Beersheba on March 29. At least one person was injured; hazmat teams were deployed amid fears of toxic chemical release from the large industrial complex, which houses Israel's most significant concentration of chemical manufacturing and hazardous waste treatment facilities. The IDF Home Front Command confirmed the impact and ordered evacuation of the immediate surrounding area as a precautionary measure. The strike followed the previous day's Iranian missile penetration at Eshtaol and represented continued evidence of Israeli air defense gaps against the Iranian ballistic missile campaign. The IDF and Israeli Fire and Rescue Service confirmed the incident.

sources.name: CNN — Iran war live updates, March 29, 2026
Al Jazeera — Day 30 of US-Israel war on Iran, March 29, 2026

media.caption: Iranian ballistic missile strikes Ne'ot Hovav chemical industrial park

title: Israel Bars Latin Patriarch from Palm Sunday Mass at Church of Holy Sepulchre; EU Condemns
detail: Israel barred the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem from celebrating Palm Sunday Mass at the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem on March 29, citing a nationwide ban on large public gatherings under emergency protocols linked to ongoing Iranian missile strikes. EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas condemned the decision as 'a violation of religious freedom,' stating it was the first time in modern history that the Latin Patriarch had been prevented from conducting Palm Sunday services at Christianity's holiest site. The incident occurred on the same day as the second consecutive Friday closure of the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound — drawing simultaneous condemnation from Muslim and Christian religious leaders. The Israeli closure of major religious sites during the Iran war has intensified criticism from European governments and human rights organizations about the cumulative erosion of religious freedom in Jerusalem.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Day 30 of US-Israel war on Iran, March 29, 2026

title: Iran Launches Six Missile Salvos at Israel; Sirens Across Central Israel and West Bank
detail: Iran launched six separate missile salvos targeting Israel on March 29 as the U.S.-Israel war on Iran entered its second month. Air raid sirens sounded across central Israel and the West Bank; medics responded to an impact at an industrial zone near Beersheba where no immediate injuries were reported. Israeli air defense systems — including Arrow 2/3 and David's Sling batteries — were activated throughout the day. The multi-salvo approach appeared designed to overwhelm and exhaust Israeli air defense capacity. The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed by Iranian action, disrupting global oil markets. The IDF and Home Front Command urged Israelis to remain near protected spaces.

sources.name: CNN — Iran war live updates, March 29, 2026
Al Jazeera — As war on Iran enters second month, Houthis open new front, March 29, 2026

media.caption: Iran launches six missile salvos at Israel; sirens across central Israel

title: Houthis Launch Second Wave of Drone and Missile Attacks on Israel Within 24 Hours
detail: Yemen's Houthi movement launched a second wave of missiles and drones targeting Israel on March 29 — less than 24 hours after their first salvo since resuming operations on March 28. Israeli air defense systems intercepted the projectiles with no casualties reported. Houthi military spokesman Yahya Saree stated the attacks targeted 'sensitive Israeli military sites' in southern Israel and pledged continued operations in solidarity with Iran, Gaza, and Lebanon 'until all declared objectives are achieved.' The dual Houthi attacks within 24 hours mark a significant reopening of the Yemeni front and mirror the Houthi attack campaign of 2023–2024 that preceded the November 2025 ceasefire freeze. Analysts warned that resumed Houthi activity raises renewed risks of Red Sea and Bab al-Mandab commercial shipping disruption.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — As war on Iran enters second month, Houthis open new front, March 29, 2026

title: Hezbollah Fires Rockets at Tiberias and Northern Israel in Coordinated Multi-Front Attack
detail: Hezbollah launched rocket fire at Tiberias (Sea of Galilee) and other northern Israeli towns on March 29, coordinating the barrage to coincide with Iran's six missile salvos in a multi-front assault designed to stretch Israeli air defense capacity. Sirens sounded across the Galilee region; air defense systems were activated. No immediate casualties were reported from the Hezbollah rockets. The simultaneous engagement from three directions — Hezbollah from Lebanon in the north, Iran from the east, and Houthis from Yemen in the south — represented a qualitative escalation in operational coordination among Iran's 'Axis of Resistance' partners. Israel's northern front with Lebanon has remained active since October 7, 2023, with IDF and Hezbollah exchanging fire intermittently throughout the 2024-2025 period.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Live coverage of Iran-Israel war, March 29, 2026

media.caption: Hezbollah fires rockets at Tiberias and northern Israel
